key_changes: Initial prompt: 'Generate a complete Cache verification environment'
Focus on basic generator, scoreboard, reference model
Added: 'Include CRV with address concentration to trigger replacement'
Added: 'Implement directed sequences for partial writes and line boundaries'
Critical: 'Implement Scoreboard using independent verification methodology'
Critical: 'Track all WRITE operations and verify with independent oracle'
verification_space_expansion: Write data tracking with mask awareness
prompt_iterations.measurable_improvement: Scoreboard now detects 4 classes of faults

key_changes: Added: 'Implement Mock DUT with realistic Cache behavior (way limit, LRU, writeback)'
Added: 'Ensure Mock DUT matches real NutShell Cache characteristics'
prompt_iterations.measurable_improvement: Mock DUT behavior matches real Cache

key_changes: Critical: 'Analyze RTL microarchitecture for potential bugs'
Critical: 'Focus on LRU implementation and dirty eviction state machine'
prompt_iterations.measurable_improvement: 3 RTL design defects discovered and documented

key_changes: Added: 'Implement end-to-end fault detection using good_ref + faulty_ref + ScoreboardMismatch'
Added: 'Ensure faults are injected in DUT response path, not just comparison'
prompt_iterations.measurable_improvement: All 5 fault types detected through ScoreboardMismatch

key_changes: Added: 'Implement OOO Scoreboard with independent writeback event stream'
Added: 'Track writeback events separately from CPU responses'
prompt_iterations.measurable_improvement: Scoreboard now handles pipelined cache responses

key_changes: Added: 'Extend coverage model with cross-coverage bins'
Added: 'Include writeback address corruption fault injection'
Added: 'Implement boundary tests for cross-line and multi-set scenarios'

bug_detections.description: Scoreboard circular reasoning: comparing ref.access() to itself
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: AI generated Scoreboard that called ref.access(txn) and compared the result to another call of ref.access(txn), making it impossible to detect any faults
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Test case: inject bit-flip in response data, Scoreboard failed to detect
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Refactor to use good_ref + faulty_ref pattern; Scoreboard compares expected (good) vs actual (faulty)

bug_detections.description: Mock DUT infinite way: never evicts, no LRU state machine
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: AI generated Mock DUT with dictionary storage that grew indefinitely without way-associativity constraints
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Test case: fill > ways addresses, all were cached without eviction
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Implement way limit, LRU tracking, dirty writeback, and memory fill on miss

bug_detections.description: Fault detection definitionally true: flipping expected then comparing to expected
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: AI generated fault detectors that modified expected values and then compared them to the original, guaranteeing mismatch regardless of actual DUT behavior
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Test: remove DUT, detectors still 'detected' faults
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Inject faults into faulty_ref access path; compare via ScoreboardMismatch

bug_detections.description: LRU round-robin bug in RTL: pseudo-LRU instead of true LRU
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: RTL implements LRU using round-robin counter instead of true recency tracking
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Reference Model vs RTL comparison: different victim selection under re-access patterns
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Implement true LRU recency stack in RTL

bug_detections.description: Dirty eviction deadlock: fill state machine doesn't handle writeback completion
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: RTL fill state machine doesn't wait for writeback to complete before proceeding
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Test: dirty eviction followed by new request causes hang
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Add writeback completion signal to fill state machine

bug_detections.description: Write-miss data loss: fill only installs memory data, doesn't merge CPU write data
bug_detections.root_cause_analysis: RTL fill state machine writes backfill data without considering the CPU write that triggered the miss
bug_detections.verification_evidence: Test: write-miss with specific data, subsequent read returns wrong value
bug_detections.fix_recommendation: Merge CPU write data/mask into fill data before installing

causal_links.description: v3 prompt explicitly required independent oracle, revealing Scoreboard circular reasoning
v4 prompt required way limits and LRU, revealing infinite way bug
v5 prompt required RTL analysis, revealing pseudo-LRU implementation
v5 prompt focused on dirty eviction, revealing deadlock scenario
v5 prompt required write-miss validation, revealing data loss bug
v6 prompt required DUT-level fault injection, revealing definitionally-true detectors
